code-scanning.title: List code scanning alerts for an enterprise

parameters.name: enterprise
parameters.description: The slug version of the enterprise name.

parameters.name: tool_name
parameters.description: The name of a code scanning tool. Only results by this tool will be listed. You can specify the tool by using either tool_name or tool_guid, but not both.

schema.description: The name of the tool used to generate the code scanning analysis.

parameters.name: tool_guid
parameters.description: The GUID of a code scanning tool. Only results by this tool will be listed. Note that some code scanning tools may not include a GUID in their analysis data. You can specify the tool by using either tool_guid or tool_name, but not both.

schema.description: The GUID of the tool used to generate the code scanning analysis, if provided in the uploaded SARIF data.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: A cursor, as given in the Link header. If specified, the query only searches for results before this cursor. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: A cursor, as given in the Link header. If specified, the query only searches for results after this cursor. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number of the results to fetch. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: direction
parameters.description: The direction to sort the results by.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: If specified, only code scanning alerts with this state will be returned.

schema.description: State of a code scanning alert.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: The property by which to sort the results.

parameters.name: assignees
parameters.description: Filter alerts by assignees. Provide a comma-separated list of user handles (e.g., octocat or octocat,hubot). Use * to list alerts with at least one assignee or none to list alerts with no assignees.

code-scanning.descriptionHTML:
Lists code scanning alerts for the default branch for all eligible repositories in an enterprise. Eligible repositories are repositories that are owned by organizations that you own or for which you are a security manager. For more information, see "Managing security managers in your organization."

The authenticated user must be a member of the enterprise to use this endpoint.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the security_events or repo scope to use this endpoint.

rule.description: Arbitrary file write during zip extraction
rule.name: js/zipslip

message.text: This path depends on a user-provided value.

example.dismissed_comment: This alert is not actually correct, because there's a sanitizer included in the library.

rule.description: Arbitrary file write during zip extraction
rule.name: js/zipslip

message.text: This path depends on a user-provided value.

created_at.description: The time that the alert was created in ISO 8601 format: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`.

updated_at.description: The time that the alert was last updated in ISO 8601 format: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`.

url.description: The REST API URL of the alert resource.

html_url.description: The GitHub URL of the alert resource.

instances_url.description: The REST API URL for fetching the list of instances for an alert.

state.description: State of a code scanning alert.

fixed_at.description: The time that the alert was no longer detected and was considered fixed in ISO 8601 format: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`.

dismissed_at.description: The time that the alert was dismissed in ISO 8601 format: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`.

dismissed_reason.description: **Required when the state is dismissed.** The reason for dismissing or closing the alert.

dismissed_comment.description: The dismissal comment associated with the dismissal of the alert.

id.description: A unique identifier for the rule used to detect the alert.

name.description: The name of the rule used to detect the alert.

security_severity_level.description: The security severity of the alert.

description.description: A short description of the rule used to detect the alert.

full_description.description: A description of the rule used to detect the alert.

tags.description: A set of tags applicable for the rule.

help.description: Detailed documentation for the rule as GitHub Flavored Markdown.

help_uri.description: A link to the documentation for the rule used to detect the alert.

name.description: The name of the tool used to generate the code scanning analysis.

version.description: The version of the tool used to generate the code scanning analysis.

guid.description: The GUID of the tool used to generate the code scanning analysis, if provided in the uploaded SARIF data.

ref.description:
The Git reference, formatted as `refs/pull/<number>/merge`, `refs/pull/<number>/head`,
`refs/heads/<branch name>` or simply `<branch name>`.

analysis_key.description: Identifies the configuration under which the analysis was executed. For example, in GitHub Actions this includes the workflow filename and job name.

environment.description: Identifies the variable values associated with the environment in which the analysis that generated this alert instance was performed, such as the language that was analyzed.

category.description: Identifies the configuration under which the analysis was executed. Used to distinguish between multiple analyses for the same tool and commit, but performed on different languages or different parts of the code.

state.description: State of a code scanning alert.

location.description: Describe a region within a file for the alert.

classifications.description:
Classifications that have been applied to the file that triggered the alert.
For example identifying it as documentation, or a generated file.

items.description: A classification of the file. For example to identify it as generated.

id.description: A unique identifier of the repository.

node_id.description: The GraphQL identifier of the repository.

name.description: The name of the repository.

full_name.description: The full, globally unique, name of the repository.

html_url.description: The URL to view the repository on GitHub.com.

fork.description: Whether the repository is a fork.

url.description: The URL to get more information about the repository from the GitHub API.

archive_url.description: A template for the API URL to download the repository as an archive.

assignees_url.description: A template for the API URL to list the available assignees for issues in the repository.

blobs_url.description: A template for the API URL to create or retrieve a raw Git blob in the repository.

branches_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about branches in the repository.

collaborators_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about collaborators of the repository.

comments_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about comments on the repository.

commits_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about commits on the repository.

compare_url.description: A template for the API URL to compare two commits or refs.

contents_url.description: A template for the API URL to get the contents of the repository.

contributors_url.description: A template for the API URL to list the contributors to the repository.

deployments_url.description: The API URL to list the deployments of the repository.

downloads_url.description: The API URL to list the downloads on the repository.

events_url.description: The API URL to list the events of the repository.

forks_url.description: The API URL to list the forks of the repository.

git_commits_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about Git commits of the repository.

git_refs_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about Git refs of the repository.

git_tags_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about Git tags of the repository.

issue_comment_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about issue comments on the repository.

issue_events_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about issue events on the repository.

issues_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about issues on the repository.

keys_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about deploy keys on the repository.

labels_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about labels of the repository.

languages_url.description: The API URL to get information about the languages of the repository.

merges_url.description: The API URL to merge branches in the repository.

milestones_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about milestones of the repository.

notifications_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about notifications on the repository.

pulls_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about pull requests on the repository.

releases_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about releases on the repository.

stargazers_url.description: The API URL to list the stargazers on the repository.

statuses_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about statuses of a commit.

subscribers_url.description: The API URL to list the subscribers on the repository.

subscription_url.description: The API URL to subscribe to notifications for this repository.

tags_url.description: The API URL to get information about tags on the repository.

teams_url.description: The API URL to list the teams on the repository.

trees_url.description: A template for the API URL to create or retrieve a raw Git tree of the repository.

hooks_url.description: The API URL to list the hooks on the repository.

code-scanning.title: List code scanning alerts for an organization

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: tool_name
parameters.description: The name of a code scanning tool. Only results by this tool will be listed. You can specify the tool by using either tool_name or tool_guid, but not both.

schema.description: The name of the tool used to generate the code scanning analysis.

parameters.name: tool_guid
parameters.description: The GUID of a code scanning tool. Only results by this tool will be listed. Note that some code scanning tools may not include a GUID in their analysis data. You can specify the tool by using either tool_guid or tool_name, but not both.

schema.description: The GUID of the tool used to generate the code scanning analysis, if provided in the uploaded SARIF data.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: A cursor, as given in the Link header. If specified, the query only searches for results before this cursor. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: A cursor, as given in the Link header. If specified, the query only searches for results after this cursor. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number of the results to fetch. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: direction
parameters.description: The direction to sort the results by.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: If specified, only code scanning alerts with this state will be returned.

schema.description: State of a code scanning alert.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: The property by which to sort the results.

parameters.name: severity
parameters.description: If specified, only code scanning alerts with this severity will be returned.

schema.description: Severity of a code scanning alert.

parameters.name: assignees
parameters.description: Filter alerts by assignees. Provide a comma-separated list of user handles (e.g., octocat or octocat,hubot). Use * to list alerts with at least one assignee or none to list alerts with no assignees.

code-scanning.descriptionHTML:
Lists code scanning alerts for the default branch for all eligible repositories in an organization. Eligible repositories are repositories that are owned by organizations that you own or for which you are a security manager. For more information, see "Managing security managers in your organization."

The authenticated user must be an owner or security manager for the organization to use this endpoint.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the security_events or repos cope to use this endpoint with private or public repositories, or the public_repo scope to use this endpoint with only public repositories.

code-scanning.title: List code scanning alerts for a repository

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: tool_name
parameters.description: The name of a code scanning tool. Only results by this tool will be listed. You can specify the tool by using either tool_name or tool_guid, but not both.

schema.description: The name of the tool used to generate the code scanning analysis.

parameters.name: tool_guid
parameters.description: The GUID of a code scanning tool. Only results by this tool will be listed. Note that some code scanning tools may not include a GUID in their analysis data. You can specify the tool by using either tool_guid or tool_name, but not both.

schema.description: The GUID of the tool used to generate the code scanning analysis, if provided in the uploaded SARIF data.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number of the results to fetch. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: ref
parameters.description: The Git reference for the results you want to list. The ref for a branch can be formatted either as refs/heads/<branch name> or simply <branch name>. To reference a pull request use refs/pull/<number>/merge.

schema.description:
The Git reference, formatted as `refs/pull/<number>/merge`, `refs/pull/<number>/head`,
`refs/heads/<branch name>` or simply `<branch name>`.

parameters.name: pr
parameters.description: The number of the pull request for the results you want to list.

parameters.name: direction
parameters.description: The direction to sort the results by.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: A cursor, as given in the Link header. If specified, the query only searches for results before this cursor. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: A cursor, as given in the Link header. If specified, the query only searches for results after this cursor. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: The property by which to sort the results.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: If specified, only code scanning alerts with this state will be returned.

schema.description: State of a code scanning alert.

parameters.name: severity
parameters.description: If specified, only code scanning alerts with this severity will be returned.

schema.description: Severity of a code scanning alert.

parameters.name: assignees
parameters.description: Filter alerts by assignees. Provide a comma-separated list of user handles (e.g., octocat or octocat,hubot). Use * to list alerts with at least one assignee or none to list alerts with no assignees.

code-scanning.descriptionHTML:
Lists code scanning alerts.

The response includes a most_recent_instance object. This provides details of the most recent instance of this alert for the default branch (or for the specified Git reference if you used ref in the request).

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the security_events scope to use this endpoint with private or public repositories, or the public_repo scope to use this endpoint with only public repositories.

code-scanning.title: Get a code scanning alert

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: alert_number
parameters.description: The number that identifies an alert. You can find this at the end of the URL for a code scanning alert within GitHub, and in the number field in the response from the GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/code-scanning/alerts operation.

schema.description: The security alert number.

code-scanning.descriptionHTML:
Gets a single code scanning alert.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the security_events scope to use this endpoint with private or public repositories, or the public_repo scope to use this endpoint with only public repositories.

example.dismissed_comment: This alert is not actually correct, because there's a sanitizer included in the library.

rule.description: Arbitrary file write during zip extraction ("Zip Slip")
rule.name: js/zipslip
rule.full_description: Extracting files from a malicious zip archive without validating that the destination file path is within the destination directory can cause files outside the destination directory to be overwritten.
rule.help: # Arbitrary file write during zip extraction ("Zip Slip")\nExtracting files from a malicious zip archive without validating that the destination file path is within the destination directory can cause files outside the destination directory to be overwritten ...

code-scanning.title: Update a code scanning alert

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: alert_number
parameters.description: The number that identifies an alert. You can find this at the end of the URL for a code scanning alert within GitHub, and in the number field in the response from the GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/code-scanning/alerts operation.

schema.description: The security alert number.

bodyParameters.name: state
bodyParameters.description: Sets the state of the code scanning alert. You must provide dismissed_reason when you set the state to dismissed.

bodyParameters.name: dismissed_reason
bodyParameters.description: Required when the state is dismissed. The reason for dismissing or closing the alert.

bodyParameters.name: dismissed_comment
bodyParameters.description: The dismissal comment associated with the dismissal of the alert.

bodyParameters.name: create_request
bodyParameters.description: If true, attempt to create an alert dismissal request.

bodyParameters.name: assignees
bodyParameters.description: The list of users to assign to the code scanning alert. An empty array unassigns all previous assignees from the alert.

code-scanning.descriptionHTML: Updates the status of a single code scanning alert. OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the security_events scope to use this endpoint with private or public repositories, or the public_repo scope to use this endpoint with only public repositories.

rule.description: Arbitrary file write during zip extraction ("Zip Slip")
rule.name: js/zipslip
rule.full_description: Extracting files from a malicious zip archive without validating that the destination file path is within the destination directory can cause files outside the destination directory to be overwritten.

dismissed_at.description: The time that the alert was dismissed in ISO 8601 format: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`.

category.description: Identifies the configuration under which the analysis was executed. Used to distinguish between multiple analyses for the same tool and commit, but performed on different languages or different parts of the code.

code-scanning.descriptionHTML:
Lists the details of all code scanning analyses for a repository, starting with the most recent. The response is paginated and you can use the page and per_page parameters to list the analyses you're interested in. By default 30 analyses are listed per page.

The rules_count field in the response give the number of rules that were run in the analysis. For very old analyses this data is not available, and 0 is returned in this field.

Warning

Closing down notice: The tool_name field is closing down and will, in future, not be included in the response for this endpoint. The example response reflects this change. The tool name can now be found inside the tool field.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the security_events scope to use this endpoint with private or public repositories, or the public_repo scope to use this endpoint with only public repositories.

code-scanning.title: Get a code scanning analysis for a repository

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: analysis_id
parameters.description: The ID of the analysis, as returned from the GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/code-scanning/analyses operation.

code-scanning.descriptionHTML:
Gets a specified code scanning analysis for a repository.

The default JSON response contains fields that describe the analysis. This includes the Git reference and commit SHA to which the analysis relates, the datetime of the analysis, the name of the code scanning tool, and the number of alerts.

The rules_count field in the default response give the number of rules that were run in the analysis. For very old analyses this data is not available, and 0 is returned in this field.

This endpoint supports the following custom media types. For more information, see "Media types."

  • application/sarif+json: Instead of returning a summary of the analysis, this endpoint returns a subset of the analysis data that was uploaded. The data is formatted as SARIF version 2.1.0. It also returns additional data such as the github/alertNumber and github/alertUrl properties.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the security_events scope to use this endpoint with private or public repositories, or the public_repo scope to use this endpoint with only public repositories.

The Git reference, formatted as `refs/pull/<number>/merge`, `refs/pull/<number>/head`,
`refs/heads/<branch name>` or simply `<branch name>`.

commit_sha.description: The SHA of the commit to which the analysis you are uploading relates.

analysis_key.description: Identifies the configuration under which the analysis was executed. For example, in GitHub Actions this includes the workflow filename and job name.

environment.description: Identifies the variable values associated with the environment in which this analysis was performed.

category.description: Identifies the configuration under which the analysis was executed. Used to distinguish between multiple analyses for the same tool and commit, but performed on different languages or different parts of the code.

created_at.description: The time that the analysis was created in ISO 8601 format: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`.

results_count.description: The total number of results in the analysis.

rules_count.description: The total number of rules used in the analysis.

version.description: The version of the tool used to generate the code scanning analysis.

Response if analysis could not be processed

code-scanning.title: Delete a code scanning analysis from a repository

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: analysis_id
parameters.description: The ID of the analysis, as returned from the GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/code-scanning/analyses operation.

parameters.name: confirm_delete
parameters.description: Allow deletion if the specified analysis is the last in a set. If you attempt to delete the final analysis in a set without setting this parameter to true, you'll get a 400 response with the message: Analysis is last of its type and deletion may result in the loss of historical alert data. Please specify confirm_delete.

code-scanning.descriptionHTML:
Deletes a specified code scanning analysis from a repository.

You can delete one analysis at a time. To delete a series of analyses, start with the most recent analysis and work backwards. Conceptually, the process is similar to the undo function in a text editor.

When you list the analyses for a repository, one or more will be identified as deletable in the response:

"deletable": true

An analysis is deletable when it's the most recent in a set of analyses. Typically, a repository will have multiple sets of analyses for each enabled code scanning tool, where a set is determined by a unique combination of analysis values:

  • ref
  • tool
  • category

If you attempt to delete an analysis that is not the most recent in a set, you'll get a 400 response with the message:

Analysis specified is not deletable.

The response from a successful DELETE operation provides you with two alternative URLs for deleting the next analysis in the set: next_analysis_url and confirm_delete_url. Use the next_analysis_url URL if you want to avoid accidentally deleting the final analysis in a set. This is a useful option if you want to preserve at least one analysis for the specified tool in your repository. Use the confirm_delete_url URL if you are content to remove all analyses for a tool. When you delete the last analysis in a set, the value of next_analysis_url and confirm_delete_url in the 200 response is null.

As an example of the deletion process, let's imagine that you added a workflow that configured a particular code scanning tool to analyze the code in a repository. This tool has added 15 analyses: 10 on the default branch, and another 5 on a topic branch. You therefore have two separate sets of analyses for this tool. You've now decided that you want to remove all of the analyses for the tool. To do this you must make 15 separate deletion requests. To start, you must find an analysis that's identified as deletable. Each set of analyses always has one that's identified as deletable. Having found the deletable analysis for one of the two sets, delete this analysis and then continue deleting the next analysis in the set until they're all deleted. Then repeat the process for the second set. The procedure therefore consists of a nested loop:

Outer loop:

  • List the analyses for the repository, filtered by tool.

  • Parse this list to find a deletable analysis. If found:

    Inner loop:

    • Delete the identified analysis.
    • Parse the response for the value of confirm_delete_url and, if found, use this in the next iteration.

The above process assumes that you want to remove all trace of the tool's analyses from the GitHub user interface, for the specified repository, and it therefore uses the confirm_delete_url value. Alternatively, you could use the next_analysis_url value, which would leave the last analysis in each set undeleted to avoid removing a tool's analysis entirely.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the repo scope to use this endpoint with private or public repositories, or the public_repo scope to use this endpoint with only public repositories.

schema.title: Analysis deletion
schema.description: Successful deletion of a code scanning analysis

next_analysis_url.description: Next deletable analysis in chain, without last analysis deletion confirmation

confirm_delete_url.description: Next deletable analysis in chain, with last analysis deletion confirmation

statusCodes.description: Response if the repository is archived or if GitHub Advanced Security is not enabled for this repository

Updates a code scanning default setup configuration.

code-scanning.title: Upload an analysis as SARIF data

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

bodyParameters.name: commit_sha
bodyParameters.description: The SHA of the commit to which the analysis you are uploading relates.

bodyParameters.name: ref
bodyParameters.description: The full Git reference, formatted as refs/heads/<branch name>, refs/tags/<tag>, refs/pull/<number>/merge, or refs/pull/<number>/head.

bodyParameters.name: sarif
bodyParameters.description: A Base64 string representing the SARIF file to upload. You must first compress your SARIF file using gzip and then translate the contents of the file into a Base64 encoding string. For more information, see "SARIF support for code scanning."

bodyParameters.name: checkout_uri
bodyParameters.description: The base directory used in the analysis, as it appears in the SARIF file. This property is used to convert file paths from absolute to relative, so that alerts can be mapped to their correct location in the repository.

bodyParameters.name: started_at
bodyParameters.description: The time that the analysis run began. This is a timestamp in ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ.

bodyParameters.name: tool_name
bodyParameters.description: The name of the tool used to generate the code scanning analysis. If this parameter is not used, the tool name defaults to "API". If the uploaded SARIF contains a tool GUID, this will be available for filtering using the tool_guid parameter of operations such as GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/code-scanning/alerts.

code-scanning.descriptionHTML:
Uploads SARIF data containing the results of a code scanning analysis to make the results available in a repository. For troubleshooting information, see "Troubleshooting SARIF uploads."

There are two places where you can upload code scanning results.

  • If you upload to a pull request, for example --ref refs/pull/42/merge or --ref refs/pull/42/head, then the results appear as alerts in a pull request check. For more information, see "Triaging code scanning alerts in pull requests."
  • If you upload to a branch, for example --ref refs/heads/my-branch, then the results appear in the Security tab for your repository. For more information, see "Managing code scanning alerts for your repository."

You must compress the SARIF-formatted analysis data that you want to upload, using gzip, and then encode it as a Base64 format string. For example:

gzip -c analysis-data.sarif | base64 -w0

SARIF upload supports a maximum number of entries per the following data objects, and an analysis will be rejected if any of these objects is above its maximum value. For some objects, there are additional values over which the entries will be ignored while keeping the most important entries whenever applicable. To get the most out of your analysis when it includes data above the supported limits, try to optimize the analysis configuration. For example, for the CodeQL tool, identify and remove the most noisy queries. For more information, see "SARIF results exceed one or more limits."

SARIF dataMaximum valuesAdditional limits
Runs per file20
Results per run25,000Only the top 5,000 results will be included, prioritized by severity.
Rules per run25,000
Tool extensions per run100
Thread Flow Locations per result10,000Only the top 1,000 Thread Flow Locations will be included, using prioritization.
Location per result1,000Only 100 locations will be included.
Tags per rule20Only 10 tags will be included.

The 202 Accepted response includes an id value. You can use this ID to check the status of the upload by using it in the /sarifs/{sarif_id} endpoint. For more information, see "Get information about a SARIF upload."

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the security_events scope to use this endpoint with private or public repositories, or the public_repo scope to use this endpoint with only public repositories.

This endpoint is limited to 1,000 requests per hour for each user or app installation calling it.

id.description: An identifier for the upload.

url.description: The REST API URL for checking the status of the upload.

statusCodes.description: Bad Request if the sarif field is invalid
Response if the repository is archived or if GitHub Advanced Security is not enabled for this repository
Payload Too Large if the sarif field is too large

code-scanning.title: Get information about a SARIF upload

parameters.name: sarif_id

code-scanning.descriptionHTML: Gets information about a SARIF upload, including the status and the URL of the analysis that was uploaded so that you can retrieve details of the analysis. For more information, see "Get a code scanning analysis for a repository." OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the security_events scope to use this endpoint with private or public repositories, or the public_repo scope to use this endpoint with only public repositories.

processing_status.description: `pending` files have not yet been processed, while `complete` means results from the SARIF have been stored. `failed` files have either not been processed at all, or could only be partially processed.

analyses_url.description: The REST API URL for getting the analyses associated with the upload.

errors.description: Any errors that ocurred during processing of the delivery.

statusCodes.description: Response if GitHub Advanced Security is not enabled for this repository
Not Found if the sarif id does not match any upload

The time period to filter by.

For example, day will filter for rule suites that occurred in the past 24 hours, and week will filter for insights that occurred in the past 7 days (168 hours).

Lists dismissal requests for code scanning alerts for all repositories in an organization.

The user must be authorized to review dismissal requests for the organization. Personal access tokens (classic) need the security_events scope to use this endpoint.

name.description: The name of the organization.

requester.description: The user who requested the dismissal request.

actor_id.description: The ID of the GitHub user who requested the dismissal request.

actor_name.description: The name of the GitHub user who requested the dismissal request.

data.description: Data describing the dismissal request metadata.

reason.description: The reason for the dismissal request.

pr_review_thread_id.description: The ID of the pull request review thread.

status.description: The status of the dismissal request.

responses.description: The responses to the dismissal request.

items.title: Dismissal request response
items.description: A response made by a requester to dismiss the request.

id.description: The ID of the response to the dismissal request.

reviewer.description: The user who reviewed the dismissal request.

actor_id.description: The ID of the GitHub user who reviewed the dismissal request.

actor_name.description: The name of the GitHub user who reviewed the dismissal request.

message.description: The response comment of the reviewer.

status.description: The response status to the dismissal request until dismissed.

created_at.description: The date and time the response to the dismissal request was created.

html_url.description: The URL to view the dismissal request in a browser.

alert-dismissal-requests.title: Get a dismissal request for a code scanning alert for a repository

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: alert_number
parameters.description: The number that identifies the code scanning alert.

alert-dismissal-requests.descriptionHTML:
Gets a dismissal request to dismiss a code scanning alert in a repository.

Delegated alert dismissal must be enabled on the repository. Personal access tokens (classic) need the security_events scope to use this endpoint.

schema.title: Code scanning alert dismissal request
schema.description: Alert dismisal request made by a user asking to dismiss a code scanning alert.

id.description: The unique identifier of the dismissal request.

number.description: The number uniquely identifying the dismissal request within its repository.

repository.description: The repository the dismissal request is for.

status.description: The status of the dismissal request.

alert-dismissal-requests.title: Review a dismissal request for a code scanning alert for a repository

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: alert_number
parameters.description: The number that identifies the code scanning alert.

bodyParameters.name: status
bodyParameters.description: The review action to perform on the bypass request.

bodyParameters.name: message
bodyParameters.description: A message to include with the review. Has a maximum character length of 2048.

alert-dismissal-requests.descriptionHTML:
Approve or deny a dismissal request to dismiss a code scanning alert in a repository.

Delegated alert dismissal must be enabled on the repository and the user must be a dismissal reviewer to access this endpoint. Personal access tokens (classic) need the security_events scope to use this endpoint.

statusCodes.description: Validation failed, or the endpoint has been spammed.
